raw_consumer_statement:
I made a purchase that was posted on my statement on XX/XX/year>, for {$100.00}. The package was never delivered to me. XXXX returned the item to the merchant on XX/XX/year>. I received nothing, and XXXX still being charged. 

I originally filed a dispute with Citibank on XX/XX/XXXX, well within the 60-day timeframe. However, the merchant contacted me and promised to refund me if I canceled the dispute. I trusted them and closed the case. But they never issued the refund. 

After realizing I was misled, I contacted Citibank customer service and reopened the dispute on XX/XX/XXXX. I also submitted supporting documents, including proof that the item was returned to the merchant. 

Citibank later denied the case, claiming I submitted it too late. This is incorrect. I had filed the original dispute on time, and the re-submission was still within the allowed 60-day window. Citi is refusing to acknowledge my original timely dispute.

I now have no product and no refund. Im requesting CFPBs help to reopen this dispute and ensure Citibank handles it properly.
